behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function seam (key-derivation logic). Real failure is deterministic canonicalization/hashing errors inside the key generator, not module handoff, OS/network, or model behavior. Rejection-gate: Integration/System are disqualified because behavior text only asserts parameter->key mapping properties and does not require DB, network, subprocess, or cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit tests for the key-generation function: (1) same param sets in different ordering/serialization forms produce identical keys, (2) many distinct param combinations do not collide, (3) edge cases (nulls, arrays, unicode, numeric/string distinctions) preserve uniqueness/determinism.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function/object seam (TTL computation from category config). Failure boundary is rule lookup/math correctness. No required real infra, no multi-module boundary in the behavior text, no AI dependence. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior is computation from configured rules, which can be supplied in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests for TTL calculator with injected config map: verify exact TTL outputs per category, unknown-category fallback behavior, and boundary conditions (zero/negative/missing values) to catch misapplied freshness rules.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (memory-pressure signal + config + strategy selector). Real failure appears when modules are wired: threshold reading, comparison, and policy switch propagation. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS memory metric if usage is injected, so not System; (b) no AI output, so not Agentic; (c) no multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring cache manager, config provider, and strategy selector in-process. Drive memory usage below/above threshold and assert active policy transitions LRU->LFU exactly at configured cutoff, including hysteresis/flip-flop guard if implemented.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam with local DB interaction (write path -> invalidation path -> cache key matching). This failure typically emerges in handoff between persistence layer/change notification logic and cache invalidator. Integration-brake: local PostgreSQL test instance is sufficient and matches Integration definition (local DB). Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real network faults/OS primitives beyond local DB-module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with cache + repository + local PostgreSQL: seed rows and corresponding cache keys, perform UPDATE/INSERT/DELETE on target table, trigger invalidation path, and assert all and only matching keys are removed while unrelated keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local object seam (eviction algorithm state: recency/frequency bookkeeping and victim selection). Real failures are algorithmic ordering/counting bugs inside cache/policy object state transitions. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text does not require external module handoff; System/Agentic/Workflow are explicitly not implicated.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests on cache/policy object with fixed capacity: craft access patterns to produce unambiguous LRU and LFU victims, fill to capacity, insert one more item, and assert exact evicted key for each policy including tie-break rules.
